commands.summary: Contacts metadata, points, and policy over HTTP (CLV-009/010/011): POST /api/contacts/update, POST /api/contacts/delete, GET/POST /api/contacts/points, and GET/POST /api/contacts/policy all require the per-launch API token (401 without it) and round-trip through the database. 13/13 server tests pass.

v8 migration is now defensive: it reads PRAGMA table_info(contacts) and only ADDs columns that are absent, so a legacy-imported contacts table that already carries a `tags` column migrates without an ERR_SQLITE_ERROR duplicate-column failure. Legacy-copy and metadata round-trip tests pass (13/13).

New renderer test 'edits and deletes contact metadata (CLV-009)': opens a contact's Edit modal, fills company, selects trust level, toggles pinned, saves, and asserts the POST /api/contacts/update body; then deletes via the modal (confirm-guarded) and asserts the POST /api/contacts/delete body. 21/21 renderer tests pass; existing SMS composition tests unaffected.
Full suite green: 70 backend tests pass (1 skipped opt-in Twilio), 21 renderer tests pass.

environment.process_note: 015 Phase 3 CLV-009 (rich contact metadata). Server exposes six token-gated contact endpoints (update/delete/points x2/policy x2). The renderer adds an Edit entry point on each contact card and a contact-edit modal with name/company/role/tags/notes/trust-level fields plus pinned/favorite toggles and a confirm-guarded delete, wired through api.updateContact/deleteContact. The v8 migration was hardened to skip pre-existing columns so legacy imports migrate cleanly. CLV-009 is satisfied. CLV-010 (unknown-inbound attach/new/ignore/block) and CLV-011 (attention-policy enforcement of blocked/muted) remain pending; their data layer (contact_points, resolveContactIdByValue, contact_policy) is already in place.
